_meta.purpose: Labelled ordering-edge requirements shared by E1-E4.

fields.statement: the requirement text exactly as an author might write it
fields.value_type: for containers: the element/value type (null for scalars)
fields.order_relevant: GROUND TRUTH: does the observable output order matter to a correct impl?
fields.intended_order: GROUND TRUTH: the order a correct impl must produce (null if irrelevant)
fields.inferable: GROUND TRUTH: can a competent engineer recover intended_order from the STATEMENT ALONE (no extra context)?
fields.trap: optional: why this row stresses a naive classifier

invariants: order_relevant=true   => intended_order is a non-empty string and inferable is a boolean
if inferable=false then order_relevant must be true (only relevant orders can be non-inferable)

requirements.statement: Return the unique tags applied to a document.
Return a map from user id to that user's total score.
Return whether every item passed validation.
Return the number of currently active sessions.
Return the search results ranked by relevance.
Return the audit events in the order they occurred.
Filter the array to items that are in stock, preserving input order.
Return the top 10 posts by upvotes.
Deduplicate the list of email addresses.
requirements.intended_order: stable / keep first occurrence in input order
requirements.trap: order matters (which dup kept / sequence) but the statement names no order

requirements.statement: Return all error messages collected during parsing.
requirements.trap: callers read these top-to-bottom; encounter-order is expected but unstated

requirements.statement: Group orders by customer id.
requirements.intended_order: outer map unordered; each inner array in input order
requirements.trap: Map return type looks order-free, but the Array VALUES carry an order — naive 'Map => dismiss' is a FALSE DISMISS here

requirements.statement: Return the set of permissions granted to a role.
Merge two already-sorted lists into one sorted list.
requirements.intended_order: sorted ascending; tie-break between equal elements unspecified
requirements.trap: the SORT is inferable but the tie-break/stability between equal elements is not

requirements.statement: Return the effective configuration as an object.
requirements.trap: object key order is not semantically observable

requirements.statement: List the files in the given directory.
requirements.intended_order: unspecified (OS readdir order vs sorted is a real decision)
requirements.trap: looks trivial; the order is a genuine undecided contract

requirements.statement: Return the median of the samples.
Return the playlist tracks in shuffle order.
requirements.trap: order is relevant AND intentionally non-deterministic — a stability test would be wrong

requirements.statement: Return the distinct categories present, alphabetically.
Return the histogram bucket counts.
Return whether the user has the given permission.
Return the leaderboard.
requirements.intended_order: sorted by score descending; tie-break (e.g. earliest to reach score) unspecified
requirements.trap: primary sort inferable, tie-break not — a defect hides in equal-score ordering

requirements.statement: Collect all warnings raised during the run.
Return the path of nodes from A to B.
requirements.intended_order: sequence-significant: A first, B last, in traversal order

requirements.statement: Return the document's tags as a comma-separated string.
requirements.intended_order: deterministic tag order (e.g. sorted) so the string is stable
requirements.trap: string is an order-bearing type so type-rule keeps it (safe), but the order derives from an upstream unordered set
